title: Sentinel Shield consumer-validation record
description: One machine-readable record produced by a real-consumer validation driver (tests/prod/2NN-<stack>-consumer.sh) against a standalone consumer fixture that Sentinel Shield does NOT own. It records, per gate, whether the gate PASSED, FAILED, or was SKIPPED because a required toolchain was absent in the current runner. A 'skip' is NOT a pass: it is an explicitly deferred gate carrying a stable reason_code so CI (which has the toolchain) must still run it. The record also captures ONE mutation demonstration proving a runnable gate actually catches a regression. Validated jq-structurally here (jq -e .); ajv-validated in CI.

schema_version.description: Bump only on a breaking change to this record shape.

kind.description: The consumer SHAPE. 'php-library' is a framework-agnostic Composer package: it is explicitly NOT Laravel or Symfony.

path.description: Repo-relative path to the standalone consumer fixture.

profile.description: The Sentinel Shield profile the consumer resolves to (e.g. php_library).

toolchain.description: Presence of each required binary in THIS runner. 'absent' forces the tool's gates to 'skip'.

tool_groups.description: Each quality dimension is satisfied by exactly ONE tool from an allowed set (one-of). The consumer picks; the engine must not hard-code a single vendor.

gates.description: Every gate the driver considered, in run order, each with an explicit status. Gates that could not run because a toolchain is absent MUST appear here with status 'skip' and a TOOLCHAIN_ABSENT_* reason_code — never omitted, never silently passed.

mutation.description: ONE fault injection against a RUNNABLE gate, proving the gate is not a no-op. 'caught' is true only when observed_status == expected_status (a real failure was observed).

target_gate.description: gate.id the mutation was injected against.

result.description: 'pass' = every runnable gate passed and the mutation was caught. 'partial' = runnable gates passed but one or more gates are SKIP (deferred to CI). 'fail' = a runnable gate failed or the mutation was not caught.

limitations.description: Human-readable statements of what was NOT proven here (e.g. 'composer/php absent: phpunit/phpstan/pint deferred to CI').

selected.description: The one tool the consumer chose to satisfy this dimension.

candidates.description: The allowed one-of set for this dimension (proves the engine accepts alternatives).

reasonCode.description: Stable, greppable code. Skips MUST use a TOOLCHAIN_ABSENT_* code so CI can enumerate deferred work.
